rules: Headlines MUST use tracking-tight equivalent in React Native (letterSpacing: -0.5 to -1).
Overlines/Labels MUST be Uppercase + Tiny (10-12px) + Wide Spacing (letterSpacing: 1.5).
Minimum body text size 16px for readability.
Use explicit font weights (e.g., '300' for elegant light, '800' for impact) rather than relying strictly on 'bold'.
Generous Spacing Philosophy: Use 2-3x more spacing than typical mobile apps.
Padding inside cards must be at least 16px to 24px to prevent data collision.
Mobile-First Responsive: Ensure safe area insets are respected using react-native-safe-area-context.
Do NOT center align everything. Medical data should be strictly left-aligned for scannability.

buttons.primary: Pill-shaped (borderRadius: 9999) with Bio-Cobalt background and stark white text. No soft drop shadows.
buttons.secondary: Sharp rectangular (borderRadius: 4) with 1px border. Used for medical actions.

cards.style: The 'Grid Borders' tech look. 1px solid border with NO shadow in dark mode, extremely subtle 4px blur shadow in light mode. Medical records should look like physical lab reports.
cards.radius: Small (borderRadius: 6 or 8) - avoid overly bubbly rounded corners.

inputs.style: High contrast, 1px border. Label must be outside the input, NOT floating. Focus state must change border to primary color.

security_visuals.e2ee_badge: A small pill badge at the top of the consultation screen. Background: dark green/black mix. Text: bright green. Icon: Lock. Indicates local-first encryption.
security_visuals.simulated_stt: When recording, show a subtle pulsing waveform or a glowing mic icon (using React Native Animated API).

rules: Use React Native 'Animated' or 'react-native-reanimated' for smooth state changes.
Add scale-down on press for buttons (transform: [{ scale: 0.98 }]).
Entrance animations for lists: Staggered fade and slide up.
Simulated STT transcript should appear to type out smoothly or slide in as chunks.
WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance. Ensure contrast ratio is > 4.5:1.
All interactive elements MUST have 'accessible={true}' and appropriate 'accessibilityLabel' or 'accessibilityRole'.

icons_and_assets.instructions: Use clean, stroke-based icons. For dense medical data, use 16px or 20px icons.

image_urls.description: Serious male doctor in white coat

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL: This is a React Native Expo application. DO NOT use HTML elements (div, span, p) or CSS files like tailwind.
CRITICAL: All styling MUST be done using 'StyleSheet.create' from 'react-native'.
CRITICAL: Avoid generic rounded UI. Apply the 'Surgical Precision' theme - sharp, highly legible, left-aligned data.
CRITICAL: Implement the simulated STT with a local state array that updates sequentially to mimic real-time transcription.
CRITICAL: Add E2EE visual cues (Lock icons, 'End-to-End Encrypted' text) to patient records and consultation screens.
CRITICAL: Use 'lucide-react-native' for icons. No AI emojis.
CRITICAL: For JSON/PDF generation, implement the UI for the prescription preview and mock functions for export.
CRITICAL: Ensure the UI conveys a 'Local-First' and HIPAA compliant architecture.
CRITICAL: Do NOT use complete transparent backgrounds for overlays that have content underneath.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Don't make generic centered layouts. Left-align dense content. Create depth through layered design elements.
Every interaction needs micro-animations. Use React Native's Pressable with conditionally applied opacity or scale.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Never use generic AI emojis for icons. Use lucide-react-native.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a 'testID' attribute in React Native for testing (e.g., testID='login-form-submit-button').
GENERAL UI UX DESIGN GUIDELINES: Maintain clear visual hierarchy, keep navigation predictable, provide immediate visual feedback on touch, and prioritize content legibility.
